text.title: Content Audit Rules and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Rules for agent-assisted scoring, redirect risk management, batching large sites, and common audit mistakes.

rule.statement: Require the agent to declare confidence level per row when it has only URL/title and no body content.
rule.rationale: Agent may confidently score pages based on insufficient information; explicit confidence flags prevent false precision in low-data rows.

rule.statement: Flag agent accuracy scores on specialized content (technical, medical, legal) for human expert review.
rule.rationale: Agent accuracy judgments on specialized topics are unreliable without domain context; these categories require subject-matter expertise.

rule.statement: Batch large sites into topical groups and audit each group with domain-appropriate criteria.
rule.rationale: Context window limits mean approximately 200-400 page rows per call; consistent criteria across batches requires the same prompt for each group.

rule.statement: Generate a redirect mapping before any deletion; "Remove" recommendations must include redirect destination.
rule.rationale: Removing pages without redirects breaks inbound links and search rankings; agent should output redirect pairs, not just removal decisions.

rule.statement: Final Keep/Remove decisions must be approved by a content owner or PM, not executed automatically.
rule.rationale: Automated content deletion without human sign-off carries irreversible SEO and user-trust risk; agent output is advisory.

antipattern.description: Incomplete inventory — missing PDFs, gated content, JavaScript-rendered pages.
antipattern.reason: An audit of 70% of content produces decisions that may conflict with unreviewed items; the inventory must be complete before scoring begins.
Content decays continuously; a single audit snapshot becomes stale within months without a review schedule and owner assignments.

antipattern.description: All-or-nothing approach — auditing the entire site before taking any action.
antipattern.reason: Large audits stall on completion anxiety; start with priority areas (core pages, high-traffic content) to generate early wins.

text.reference: Merge Screaming Frog crawl CSV with GA pageview data
